_schema.description: Humanitarian AI program funding by source, 2020–2024. Each record is one bar (one year). Each category field is a numeric segment value. The chart supports both absolute stacking (total investment) and 100% stacking (proportional composition).

fields.categories[].id: string — key matching data record fields
fields.categories[].label: string — legend and tooltip label
fields.categories[].color: string — hex fill color for this segment
fields.data[].{categoryId}: number — funding value for this category in this year
fields.data[].note: string — optional annotation shown in bar tooltip

_schema.replace: Add or remove category keys across both 'categories' and 'data'. All data[].{categoryId} values must be non-negative. The chart handles normalization for 100% mode automatically.

categories.label: Government
categories.desc: Bilateral and multilateral government grants and contracts.

categories.label: Private Foundation
categories.desc: Philanthropic foundations and corporate social responsibility funds.

categories.label: NGO / Multilateral
categories.desc: International NGOs, UN agencies, and pooled humanitarian funds.

categories.label: Research / Academic
categories.desc: University partnerships, research grants, and academic collaborations.

data.note: Baseline year. COVID-19 disrupts field operations; AI tools gain traction for remote monitoring.
Recovery phase. Tech philanthropies begin significant commitments. Government budgets stabilise.
Inflection point. Ukraine crisis accelerates AI deployment for displacement tracking at scale.
Private sector surge. Major foundation commitments announced at UNGA. Research funding doubles in 2 years.
Private closes gap with Government. Total investment tripled since 2020. Portfolio diversification visible.
